name: Conservatives Give
subject: Milton Friedman: What does “created equal” mean?
body:
One of the reasons young people are so attracted to the idea of socialism is the promised equality of outcomes.

One of the reasons young people are so attracted to the idea of socialism is the promised equality of outcomes.
| | | [](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=73b6e32ab00c4cf1b489cddc77b45c38&_e=P6Pwokxrgp07e_JQ5kOjXd5898R71hSFuhvBXItypVzE-W4vY6T6rWToZkBR9vU26G9XV78DBz-Afd3BuroOKox2HXbp0BhqDbiVZ6cdQkjoyDSVap6gIblQIFPVqVX5grC-EblBfwobLb0uxq1zeMCv1Calfzrb1H94HkFgBDRISd0IdH-L3Imaw2QKeO4i3iUCwtvfjl0r1y89rWeN9-fM8I62q5e9Bw0yJGC6aDRFtiHQ-B0uf1kjAbex_U59SVIbVtJgCIvcBvp043vwKT2GKosUWuFG2jlV2Z9zcP_1A-l3-AoZiX89nadEMPeSD1x-RaPbxepxH0HoyQDYboABSp3rW_JoLDtpYfxT_3s837tH-RJxUvd3X2FI7pOH)
---
| | Hi Friend, One of the reasons young people are so attracted to the idea of socialism is the promised equality of outcomes.

They want life to be fair for everyone, and for all to have wellness, happiness, and financial security. But if the end goal of socialism is "equality", that begs the question: what is equality? Are equality and liberty consistent one with the other or are they in conflict?
---
| [](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=73b6e32ab00c4cf1b489cddc77b45c38&_e=P6Pwokxrgp07e_JQ5kOjXd5898R71hSFuhvBXItypVzE-W4vY6T6rWToZkBR9vU26G9XV78DBz-Afd3BuroOKox2HXbp0BhqDbiVZ6cdQkjoyDSVap6gIblQIFPVqVX5grC-EblBfwobLb0uxq1zeMCv1Calfzrb1H94HkFgBDRISd0IdH-L3Imaw2QKeO4i3iUCwtvfjl0r1y89rWeN94zMpTh67-gofzNAcdo-tOs-T8pTdTI6qsnZ5eqzU5GZ8ZXXGgwfC5rDRs4lKGV56XW-ic7ApQVqpskcVT0y24SueiCkaq2ALcC3bS2MsHst1GtqXl7GE_eDbv3fziP4IvJ5kuzWR7loUBxS3oH2tHtwwrS33iSXErru3I6Pl5UO)
---
| | In this **[[25-page commentary](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=73b6e32ab00c4cf1b489cddc77b45c38&_e=P6Pwokxrgp07e_JQ5kOjXd5898R71hSFuhvBXItypVzE-W4vY6T6rWToZkBR9vU26G9XV78DBz-Afd3BuroOKox2HXbp0BhqDbiVZ6cdQkjoyDSVap6gIblQIFPVqVX5grC-EblBfwobLb0uxq1zeMCv1Calfzrb1H94HkFgBDRISd0IdH-L3Imaw2QKeO4i3iUCwtvfjl0r1y89rWeN99ssaibpMR-w0QE0O9U_ii8zhrBBsPsDskx4TNpqKsFb1CVbIF2h5E-EmbJwXhiFtcLWb09rlJAmfUEBbfV0XJDEAPcjSmkofizCGK3Xtcl-xPkNZOdk62Efvbsw2Fw3gCsZ4oQLKoY26XMIK7aeqrrR8qyA9-abWdhPvBJCzv52)](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=73b6e32ab00c4cf1b489cddc77b45c38&_e=P6Pwokxrgp07e_JQ5kOjXd5898R71hSFuhvBXItypVzE-W4vY6T6rWToZkBR9vU26G9XV78DBz-Afd3BuroOKox2HXbp0BhqDbiVZ6cdQkjoyDSVap6gIblQIFPVqVX5grC-EblBfwobLb0uxq1zeMCv1Calfzrb1H94HkFgBDRISd0IdH-L3Imaw2QKeO4i3iUCwtvfjl0r1y89rWeN9wVFhxjmyH1Ttk_LWVOJg4SxeaYCYHDjxx5PxfI-IZOorruOnzClvZrLgz7lEUCr-lDn0ZnBzBg2Ij5-jtI6H6u0MjpnAeLoksZ8I9IJSKGHASZpfRBZkmvHFVysXpnPT89-53U-t-YxHPT8OLNzPDsBvUttMHgomuNkqU8x50PN)** , "Created Equal", legendary economist and Hoover fellow Milton Friedman answers these and other questions in great depth. You'll learn what Thomas Jefferson meant when he wrote the words "created equal" in the Declaration of Independence and how the meaning has changed through American history. You'll see how Friedman brilliantly illustrates differences between equality of opportunity and equality of outcome.

With many today striving to increase government power in the name of a concept of "equality", Friedman's words still ring true for those who believe that a society that puts freedom first will end up with both greater freedom and greater equality.

**[Please follow this link to get your free copy of "Created Equal" now.](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=73b6e32ab00c4cf1b489cddc77b45c38&_e=P6Pwokxrgp07e_JQ5kOjXd5898R71hSFuhvBXItypVzE-W4vY6T6rWToZkBR9vU26G9XV78DBz-Afd3BuroOKox2HXbp0BhqDbiVZ6cdQkjoyDSVap6gIblQIFPVqVX5grC-EblBfwobLb0uxq1zeMCv1Calfzrb1H94HkFgBDRISd0IdH-L3Imaw2QKeO4i3iUCwtvfjl0r1y89rWeN904Dbn9BSEYdfXVdNE2_9C06W4rl5tqnC7riz3bmn2W1jqTOHJ4xVPaVKImLNfWYqiVz_gYXaw5cyM1cc4GtkuOLUEbu4ee-vp3o8Z0Kon6nKRDtpONEHdOe-CVCqnlJa3gIXLQ4nDImY2VKR-a-RvCIgYEhw1padtfKR0RVySZf)**
Thanks,

Greg Greg Stamps | Online Development Hoover Institution | Stanford University

name: Dyneco Gibson
subject: Join us TODAY for an exclusive, virtual townhall
body:
| | |
---
|
---
| | Good Afternoon, We hope this invitation finds you well. The **New Pennsylvania Project** and **Why Courts Matter - PA** are excited to continue our vital work providing civic education to our centered communities. As a voting rights organization, our mission is to provide current, accurate, and accessible information about the importance of voting in every election. We’d love for you to join us today for our **Why Courts Matter- 201 Virtual Town Hall** :Date: Thursday, January 30, 2025 Time: 6:30 – 8:00 PM (via Zoom) Speakers: Kadida Kenner, Founding CEO, New Pennsylvania Project _Why Attend? _ This town hall will focus on the importance of our state and federal courts, how they impact our daily lives, and how to engage in the state judicial election process, and protect the independence of our federal courts. Please[ register today](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9YhVueAlOBMb0se9nBbj0OBKxWIYHr3D3kJ-2BAjmyFPsLum57T2GzASBq6JjnSuuR564kCs8eq92anljVtxZcRNh49sIf-2Fc9-2Fq964oFQLkAlEQi-2BsK-2Foa37TtaY31gJtcRxA-3D-3DQlbh_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYaCVsuxzJai57je-2BsyRy37tBu-2BxJi-2FE0ODLGxil4E1bLXk8pShKjWQ28KyCR2FRq0kLejQSNPqQNNNut1pzCUy7W5FemWSKZNmLbiGE9SLd5M0Clvt6bC7Vugx5MZjj0ZaL8Fn3BScjDvMBkTN8-2FUHRAfpzwi094753toASt0jNQ4-2F-2BkufauS4E3OX6QDjb2L), and feel free to share this with your friends and family.
Specifically, we will discuss:
1.- How federal judges are appointed and confirmed for a lifetime, and their long-term impact on our daily lives.
2.- The 2025 Pennsylvania judicial elections (including retention elections for three seats on the PA Supreme Court).
Your participation in state judicial elections can make a difference. A civically engaged electorate is crucial to ensuring fair and independent courts.

We look forward to having you with us as we work toward an informed and empowered electorate in 2025!

Please register at the link below, and invite a friend. We need more people to join the movement. We look forward to seeing you.
---
[ RSVP HERE](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9YhVueAlOBMb0se9nBbj0OBKxWIYHr3D3kJ-2BAjmyFPsLum57T2GzASBq6JjnSuuR564kCs8eq92anljVtxZcRNh49sIf-2Fc9-2Fq964oFQLkAlEQi-2BsK-2Foa37TtaY31gJtcRxA-3D-3D5xIX_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYaCVsuxzJai57je-2BsyRy37tBu-2BxJi-2FE0ODLGxil4E1bJ6UFBlyUCLRQQh6rxvqgLVHCLqh9CyUHz554KAPgW760m0pgF24eF-2BPggM0CLEJbyK5A0LK18I-2B6v6j42kOxcPQZC7uWFb3LpoVGAaBJYxyoWPK4abiGTyx2HNw4GLOgb-2FS-2F1ERJACAF9G7B9uT-2F8o)

clean_body: | | | | | | Good Afternoon, We hope this invitation finds you well. The New Pennsylvania Project and Why Courts Matter - PA are excited to continue our vital work providing civic education to our centered communities. As a voting rights organization, our mission is to provide current, accurate, and accessible information about the importance of voting in every election. We’d love for you to join us today for our Why Courts Matter- 201 Virtual Town Hall :Date: Thursday, January 30, 2025 Time: 6:30 – 8:00 PM (via Zoom) Speakers: Kadida Kenner, Founding CEO, New Pennsylvania Project Why Attend? This town hall will focus on the importance of our state and federal courts, how they impact our daily lives, and how to engage in the state judicial election process, and protect the independence of our federal courts. Please, and feel free to share this with your friends and family. Specifically, we will discuss: 1.- How federal judges are appointed and confirmed for a lifetime, and their long-term impact on our daily lives. 2.- The 2025 Pennsylvania judicial elections (including retention elections for three seats on the PA Supreme Court). Your participation in state judicial elections can make a difference. A civically engaged electorate is crucial to ensuring fair and independent courts. We look forward to having you with us as we work toward an informed and empowered electorate in 2025! Please register at the link below, and invite a friend. We need more people to join the movement. We look forward to seeing you. | | | In Solidarity, Dyneco Gibson Director of Campaigns and Engagementdyneco@newpaproject.org ; (717) 847-1201 The New Pennsylvania Project is a 501(c)(4) non profit organization, contributions are not for charitable purposes and are not tax deductible. | | | | | ---|---|--- | | Our Contact Information New Pennsylvania Project P.O. Box 443 West Chester, PA 19381 4126996771 | |

name: Gen Z vs Trump
subject: We’re mad at Democrats. You should be too.
body:
| |
---
The party that called Trump a fascist for years is now enabling his fascism, but we won’t stand for it. | [View in browser](https://jjajxo.clicks.mlsend.com/tj/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyODY4MDcsXCJsXCI6MTQ0OTcxNzI1MTQ3MjExMDAwLFwiclwiOjE0NDk3MTc5MjI0NDU0MTM2NH0iLCJzIjoiODc3ZTk3YmE0MzY4NzZkNCJ9)
| |
---
| [ ](https://jjajxo.clicks.mlsend.com/tj/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyODY4MDcsXCJsXCI6MTQ0OTcxNzI1MTU0NTUxMDM2LFwiclwiOjE0NDk3MTc5MjI0NDU0MTM2NH0iLCJzIjoiNWYyZjc4MTgxY2MwZmEyOCJ9)
---
|
---
Friend,

It’s no secret that Republicans have a political agenda that will strip us of our freedoms, imperil the entire planet, and put democracy as we know it on the chopping block. Their agenda is anti-LGBTQ, anti-youth, anti-woman, anti-Muslim, anti-immigrant, and anti-peace...

_We know that, but many leaders in the Democratic Party apparently do not._

Unenthusiastic speeches on the floor of Congress and half-assed tweets weakly condemning Trump’s power grabs won’t cut it. **We need a real opposition party that is willing to shout from the rooftops that this sh!t is not normal.**

|
---
| [ ](https://jjajxo.clicks.mlsend.com/tj/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyODY4MDcsXCJsXCI6MTQ0OTcxNzI1MTU3Njk2NzY5LFwiclwiOjE0NDk3MTc5MjI0NDU0MTM2NH0iLCJzIjoiNTAxYjIyZjllNmY3Zjg2MSJ9)
---
|
---
_Gun violence prevention advocate and Democratic leader Shannon Watts rightfully ripping Dems for their pathetic lack of opposition._
|
---
We endorsed Joe Biden and then Kamala Harris because we knew that the stakes of the election were far too high to have purity tests or sit out the vote. **But we were serious when we called Trump a fascist and compared him to vicious dictators throughout history. It seems that Democratic leaders weren’t.**

As Joe Biden shook Trump’s hand and led him into the White House on Inauguration Day, news broke that Biden had just preemptively pardoned his siblings and his in-laws, fearing retribution by Trump. __How can you reasonably think Trump will go after your family on (forgive us for the pun) trumped-up charges and then smile and applaud at his inauguration?__ To us, it’s truly unfathomable.

The era of respectability politics must come to an end. We can no longer shake hands with our oppressors as they take away our livelihoods and our futures. **That’s why we’re fighting back against Trump and MAGA Republicans, even if Democratic Party leadership won’t.**__
|
---
_**[Path to Progress is the only organization led by Generation Z that engages our peers with policy-based solutions instead of political bickering. In order to fuel massive public support for our policy agenda, we’re asking grassroots supporters like you to contribute $27, or whatever you can spare today.](https://jjajxo.clicks.mlsend.com/tj/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyODY4MDcsXCJsXCI6MTQ0OTcxNzI1MTYxODkxMDc3LFwiclwiOjE0NDk3MTc5MjI0NDU0MTM2NH0iLCJzIjoiOTI4YzNhNWFiYzdjZmI3ZCJ9)**_
|
---
| | [ Donate $27 >> ](https://jjajxo.clicks.mlsend.com/tj/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyODY4MDcsXCJsXCI6MTQ0OTcxNzI1MTY2MDg1Mzg1LFwiclwiOjE0NDk3MTc5MjI0NDU0MTM2NH0iLCJzIjoiMjRlMjc0ZmM5MTIzNTg2ZCJ9)
---
|
---
| | [ Donate another amount >> ](https://jjajxo.clicks.mlsend.com/tj/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyODY4MDcsXCJsXCI6MTQ0OTcxNzI1MTY5MjMxMTE3LFwiclwiOjE0NDk3MTc5MjI0NDU0MTM2NH0iLCJzIjoiMjM4YTExMjdlZDA5MTM1ZSJ9)
---
|
---
_**[_Or multiply your impact with a monthly recurring donation!_](https://jjajxo.clicks.mlsend.com/tj/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyODY4MDcsXCJsXCI6MTQ0OTcxNzI1MTcyMzc2ODQ5LFwiclwiOjE0NDk3MTc5MjI0NDU0MTM2NH0iLCJzIjoiMDk1ZTFhNDAzMTQ0YTI0OCJ9)**_
|
---
We hope we can all agree that Democrats need to follow young people’s lead and behave like an actual opposition party. Pundits are already saying there will be no resistance this time around. We’re here to prove them wrong.

Thank you for your support,

Sam Weinberg
**Executive Director**
**Path to Progress** __
|
---
|
##### Path to Progress Action
_Checks can be sent to_ : Path to Progress Action PO Box 34004Washington, D.C.
United States of America |

ACROSS THE COMMONWEALTH
VOTE TODAY! [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93159/images/Make%20A%20Plan%20To%20Vote.png]
You can vote for November's elections NOW! Vote at your Registrar's office during business hours. Saturday early voting is available October 28th and November 4th. Learn More [https://vote.elections.virginia.gov/VoterInformation/PublicContactLookup]
Find your local Registrar's office here: https://vote.elections.virginia.gov/VoterInformation/PublicContactLookup [https://vote.elections.virginia.gov/VoterInformation/PublicContactLookup]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93159/images/VWCC.jpeg] 15 years of the Community College Access Program at VWCC
Learn More [https://www.wdbj7.com/2023/09/26/virginia-western-celebrates-15th-anniversary-ccap-program/] CCAP is a successful public/private partnership that pays the cost of tuition for three years at Virginia Western Community College if a qualified student does not have sufficient financial aid to pay tuition costs.
This program has helped over 4,200 students achieve higher education!
CANDIDATE SPOTLIGHT
https://karenforvirginia.com/ [https://karenforvirginia.com/] Karen Jenkins for HB-89
Donate Here [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/karenforvirginia] Karen Jenkins is a life-long resident of the City of Suffolk. Throughout her lifetime she has passionately advocated for others and currently serves on the Suffolk School Board with a proven track record and history of accomplishments. She is running for the House of Delegates for the 89th District to continue delivering results for the people of Virginia.
She is a champion for education, public safety and mental health. As your next Delegate for the 89th District, she will continue to fight and vote for meaningful policies and legislation to support the needs of the district. At the state level, she will be positioned to write and sponsor bills and laws to benefit the schools and communities in the 89th District and throughout the commonwealth.
LOCAL CANDIDATE EVENTS
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93159/images/lily%20canvass.png]
Canvass for Lily Franklin!
On Sundays, join Team Lily at the South County Library Parking Lot from 12 - 12:30 pm for a Roanoke canvassing event!
Bring your walking shoes, a charged cell phone, and water. When you arrive to their launch location you will receive a brief training and literature before being sent out to turf. Questions? Email Beth at egunter3@gmail.com [egunter3@gmail.com] or call 540-353-3645.
South County Library Parking Lot, 6303 Merriman Rd, Roanoke, VA 24018
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93159/images/sam%20and%20trish.jpg]
Canvass for Sam and Trish!

Join us Sunday to canvass for Sam and Trish! Meet our Field Coordinator, Dom, on September 3rd at the Williamson Road Library, 12:00pm.
Located at 3837 Williamson Rd, Roanoke, VA 24012.

Door knocking will begin about 12:30 pm!
Read More [https://www.facebook.com/events/275519338709424]
Roanoke EnVision Center Kids Movie Night - Sept. 28 Fall Harvest Festival
The movie is free and there will be snacks and much more! Fall into wellness at the Harvest Festival!

Carilion Clinic vs. Lewis Gale Charity Softball Game - Oct. 1 Henrietta Lacks Statue Unveiling - Oct. 4
Join us for the 6th Annual Carilion Clinic vs. Lewis Gale Charity Softball Game, being held at the Home of the Salem Red Sox - Carilion Clinic Field. A dignified sculpture will commemorate the ongoing and long unrecognized contributions of Roanoke native Henrietta Lacks, who unknowingly provided the world with the HeLa cell, fueling decades of life-saving and life changing research.
Physicians, nurses and administrators will suit up for each hospital during the nine-inning contest. Proceeds will benefit the American Heart Association. Unveiling is October 4th at 11 am at Henrietta Lacks Plaza.
Admission is $5 at the gate, kids under 10 free. Gates open at 1pm and the game starts at 2pm. 410 3rd St SW, Roanoke, VA 24011

Join us for a social hour every first Thursday of the month at Leonore Restaurant, 128 Campbell St. SW, 5-7 pm.
Join Roanoke Young Dems! [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93159/images/RYD.png]
Meetings occur on the first Monday and third Sunday of every month in person and virtually. The goal of RYD is to help improve our community and to promote the names of Democratic candidates and policies.

# Governor Mike Braun: State of Indiana is ‘strong’ in first address to Indiana General Assembly
---
| | [](http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/4X5cyvqn4eLxeXNTNbVutV5o1NZf_SrbQuJMepoqUp5POZg4oNYpsjk4fSrnI3fw2Cy0mJfjh1QI35kmMXcJN7iIS9rJK6Ju7kOU53yZID3PvVg0tbqfxM9ltTPLeI3S2eMHoADy_bPYw8jOS_rgfnNbCYBJz-5xYmRPVoTv_V0tvWFtQtincPcrO-MFYXm0c6lKIho6gkFBNcHb0Pr_JQoDJvVxtXoO9EgWlDKhbeG9Kj9PXoSYah37gtzASkyUOGAxUFnErJt6yhQlsf58fA8vE7WdmrGBC32heucoesEao_mK7EnL1uM8GWyrdUu-XWL_rel1f12QHpk0wLd0WGQ4kXKrAQVguwb6bnh9qAE/bw19kTxFqnqrYGewJY0iriSzZ2NCbVa_/5)
---
Fox 59
David Gay
January 29, 2025
[https://fox59.com/indianapolitics/indiana-gov-mike-braun-to-deliver-first-state-of-the-state-address/](http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/sojSBEmYgvrb2pr74OdAU3rWLQqj-wdg7SnpSF6TtisEuDYgkHotYr-TdJBSti5JGiV3MFVwjkStuIsS3u9moyG3oLOv4y5n9cQc2T_TKyon3Z4lYxXpph5N5mSDzMDRvrOXrdlYfMLtq1-dctpgjaD3oH-k6RUQoNkyoirIGpxzxFuq_Mdmzex6AVdomn_KJnyWLR-cEhwTwjTa2k8Gb52NzwqlQQlEfQmCyjmE7LfPwuSabu9KIYU0krAgq9kDko6Xa9NSXCtyoQKDBWJlPc4-9ebOvJtdwc11PpIejAQ9pUoeex1xD8su0WA4cYbAz0b6-qbD9yalI5xr7WwH-plt6Jvqy_ypXwpiefmdCOE/GU1vzbOxtC0R0oEuFEFNpgP0w_MOv-zB/5) Just weeks into his tenure as Indiana’s new Republican governor, Mike Braun highlighted the strength of Indiana and its residents as part of his first State of the State address Wednesday evening. “The state of the state is strong because Hoosiers are strong,” Braun said in remarks provided to FOX59/CBS4. As Indiana’s former U.S. Senator, Braun highlighted his travels to each of Indiana’s 92 counties, stating that in that time, he saw many families impacted by rising prices.

During Braun’s gubernatorial campaign, he released his Freedom and Opportunity Agenda, something that was developed through his conversations with Hoosiers and learning about their circumstances. Braun said that Indiana residents have connected with that agenda, already declaring wins that he says have been made weeks into his tenure. **Property Taxes** At the center of that agenda is tax relief, a topic that Braun focused on extensively throughout his campaign. “Hoosiers from all over the state told me these tax bills keep rising, but wages aren’t keeping pace,” Braun said. “This is not sustainable.” During the speech, Braun featured the experiences of a couple from Greenwood, stating that their property tax bill on their home has more than doubled since 2021. Braun said that his property tax plan is built on three priorities, including: Cutting property taxes for Hoosier families, including new homeowners and seniors Capping the annual increase to protect homeowners and farmers Reforming the system in the long term through transparency. Braun also highlighted the importance of providing seniors with tax relief on their retirement income, as well as the creation of tax-advantaged Farm Savings Accounts for farmers, two items that Braun spoke about during the campaign. In the 2025 session, Senate Bill 1, written by Indiana State Senator Travis Holdman, centers around property tax relief. The bill, in Braun’s view, helps bring forward some of his property tax proposals, including amending homestead standard deductions, providing a maximum property tax liability credit on all taxable real property and developing a property tax transparency portal. According to the Indiana General Assembly’s website, Senate Bill 1 went through its first reading on Jan. 14 and was referred to the senate’s Committee on Tax and Fiscal Policy. Ultimately, Braun believes the Indiana government should focus on the needs of the taxpayers, stating that “all governments need to do more with less.” “Every Hoosier family and business had to tighten their belts over the last four years,” Braun said. “It’s time the government did more with less.” **Governmental Efficiency** During his time in office, Braun has already called for governmental agencies to “find big cuts in their departments” and to “cut at least a quarter of government regulations.” This includes Braun’s revamped cabinet structure, which he said makes the government operate more like a business and adds a new level of accountability. According to previous reports, the cabinet under the executive branch includes eight offices led by a secretary who reports to Braun. The eight offices include: Secretary of Management and Budget Secretary of Commerce Secretary of Health and Family Services Secretary of Public Safety Secretary of Energy and Natural Resources Secretary of Education Secretary of Transportation and Infrastructure Secretary of Business Affairs
In this portion of the speech, Braun also highlighted two executive orders he signed earlier this year, one of which ended work-from-home policies and required employees to return to the office to help “improve productivity and collaboration.” The other order eliminated diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives from the state government, something that Braun said will be replaced with a “level playing field of MEI: merit, excellence and innovation.” “Diversity and equality are hallmarks of what we believe as a country,” Braun said during the speech, “but these top-down DEI mandates led to division and inefficiency. MEI puts merit and results at the forefront, and everyone has the chance to get ahead with hard work.” **Public Safety** While Braun said it was important to make government more efficient, Braun said it was important to protect vital services, including police, fire and schools. During the speech, Braun said that police recruitment and retention for the Indiana State Police needs to be improved, including: Competitive salaries Cost of living adjustments More comprehensive benefits.

Braun also highlighted Senate Bill 324, a piece of legislation written by Indiana State Senator Aaron Freeman, that would increase penalties to crimes related to fentanyl and methamphetamine, increase penalty levels of cases involving multiple battery cases and add minimum bail requirements for the release of a “repeat violent arrestee.” According to the general assembly’s website, the first reading for Senate Bill 324 initially occurred on Jan. 13 and was referred to the senate’s Committee on Corrections and Criminal Law. On Tuesday, the committee passed the bill and reassigned it to the senate’s Committee on Appropriations. Braun also took some time to speak on the immigration-related executive order he signed on Tuesday. According to previous reports, the order directed law enforcement agencies throughout Indiana to fully cooperate with the Immigration and Customs Enforcement agency, or ICE. At the time, Braun’s office said that the order “protects the public from the threats posed by illegal aliens” in the state, specifically those with criminal histories. During Wednesday’s speech, Braun said that Indiana will do whatever it can to help secure the southern border and “end this crisis.” “Let me be clear, Indiana will not be a safe haven for illegal immigration,” Braun said during the speech. “As the Trump administration begins to return illegal aliens with criminal records to their home countries, we will assist. A state should always put the safety of its own citizens first.” **Economic Development** In relation to economic development, Braun said that the state is “taking a new approach,” focusing on building workforce skills for Indiana residents to help match the high-value and high-wage jobs that need to be filled. Braun said that one of the ways the state will aim to achieve that is through the Hoosier Workforce Investment Tax Credit, something which Braun said would provide employers with a strong tax incentive to invest in skills training and development. Braun also said that the Indiana Economic Development Corporation will be restructured to “spread the benefits of economic development programs across the state.” Braun said the IEDC will also focus on “existing employers” and build on what other agencies are doing, including the newly established Office of Entrepreneurship and Innovation. **Healthcare** Along with property taxes, the other topic that Braun focused the majority of his gubernatorial campaign on was healthcare, an issue seen by some as “too tough to tackle.” “Hoosiers pay some of the highest healthcare costs in the country,” Braun said, “but we don’t get the best healthcare results. Based on experience taking on this issue in my own business, I developed by Freedom and Opportunity healthcare plan to lower prices, improve access and promote innovation in the industry.” According to previous reports, Braun said in his agenda that he aimed to address healthcare costs by enhancing price transparency requirements, something that Braun addressed in a series of healthcare-related executive orders he signed earlier this month. Some of Braun’s other healthcare executive orders include orders that direct the Indiana Department of Health to release terminated pregnancy reports, as well as orders to tackle surprise billing and high drug prices, as well as assess waste, fraud and abuse. A series of bills, including House Bill 1003, Senate Bill 3 and House Bill 1004, have already been filed as part of this year’s session, covering topics like price transparency, fighting fraud and making sure nonprofit hospitals “actually act like nonprofits.” “Hoosiers should be able to see what they’re going to have to pay before they receive care, and a medical bill... should never come as a surprise,” Braun said during the speech. **Education** The last topic that Braun covered during his speech was the importance of universal school choice in the state of Indiana, stressing his belief that parents are in charge of their own kids’ education. “Let’s put parents in the driver’s seat and kids first with universal school choice,” Braun said during the speech. “My budget proposal has the plan to get it done and with your help, we’ll win this historic victory for Hoosier parents and kids.” Braun also highlighted two bills, Senate Bill 146 and House Bill 1637, which covered two items that Braun spoke about during his campaign: the importance of increasing teacher pay and the establishment of the Office of School Safety. To conclude the speech, Braun said that his overall message to the Indiana General Assembly, as this year’s session continues, is “one of unity.” “We were all elected by Hoosiers to get results,” Braun said. “Will we treat this moment as a plateau, or as a foundation to build something truly exceptional? Now is the time to be bold and think out of the box. We can do it through big ideas, collaboration and above all: action.”

[To Read The Full Story Click Here](http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/woz9E4nTbS-fXmeZ4pl4l8gL4fM_qMW9kLC9_VKxLpvTU4Q1-R_67EJSJEZ9s07ieAVUrOZCxkJhU2GCi-yRKTqp7OmblEdKLXBuNOEywTG0SJVlVq5cuBQF-lCqO4ZOcjwjoGtfMvOVu-0SaQSoi-ZPzDukDScZ4UI2AF71x0R2T8o3L2bsQzbHHo8xIJkvm4XHLWyXvfC7zLQXuwDOyIsvCVqM646zoxGxE4Xkq3aKdkT9PscMbojS1PNjeVoSkN1QBOropx8Kjbh8soSiIOZb91m-TVaH3FxHv0Rh4eODgyh9OE0fJ_7S-4Q6TEyzVYagjXIpk5ynryls34LzLNDiWKzQf8khCCS0sZdtWXk/r_Zmg2L5m34vgpBxR78VKtk3o2S4h0pj/5)

clean_body: | | | IN CASE YOU MISSED IT Governor Mike Braun: State of Indiana is ‘strong’ in first address to Indiana General Assembly | | Fox 59 David Gay January 29, 2025 Just weeks into his tenure as Indiana’s new Republican governor, Mike Braun highlighted the strength of Indiana and its residents as part of his first State of the State address Wednesday evening. “The state of the state is strong because Hoosiers are strong,” Braun said in remarks provided to FOX59/CBS4. As Indiana’s former U.S. Senator, Braun highlighted his travels to each of Indiana’s 92 counties, stating that in that time, he saw many families impacted by rising prices. During Braun’s gubernatorial campaign, he released his Freedom and Opportunity Agenda, something that was developed through his conversations with Hoosiers and learning about their circumstances. Braun said that Indiana residents have connected with that agenda, already declaring wins that he says have been made weeks into his tenure. Property Taxes At the center of that agenda is tax relief, a topic that Braun focused on extensively throughout his campaign. “Hoosiers from all over the state told me these tax bills keep rising, but wages aren’t keeping pace,” Braun said. “This is not sustainable.” During the speech, Braun featured the experiences of a couple from Greenwood, stating that their property tax bill on their home has more than doubled since 2021. Braun said that his property tax plan is built on three priorities, including: Cutting property taxes for Hoosier families, including new homeowners and seniors Capping the annual increase to protect homeowners and farmers Reforming the system in the long term through transparency. Braun also highlighted the importance of providing seniors with tax relief on their retirement income, as well as the creation of tax-advantaged Farm Savings Accounts for farmers, two items that Braun spoke about during the campaign. In the 2025 session, Senate Bill 1, written by Indiana State Senator Travis Holdman, centers around property tax relief. The bill, in Braun’s view, helps bring forward some of his property tax proposals, including amending homestead standard deductions, providing a maximum property tax liability credit on all taxable real property and developing a property tax transparency portal. According to the Indiana General Assembly’s website, Senate Bill 1 went through its first reading on Jan. 14 and was referred to the senate’s Committee on Tax and Fiscal Policy. Ultimately, Braun believes the Indiana government should focus on the needs of the taxpayers, stating that “all governments need to do more with less.” “Every Hoosier family and business had to tighten their belts over the last four years,” Braun said. “It’s time the government did more with less.” Governmental Efficiency During his time in office, Braun has already called for governmental agencies to “find big cuts in their departments” and to “cut at least a quarter of government regulations.” This includes Braun’s revamped cabinet structure, which he said makes the government operate more like a business and adds a new level of accountability. According to previous reports, the cabinet under the executive branch includes eight offices led by a secretary who reports to Braun. The eight offices include: Secretary of Management and Budget Secretary of Commerce Secretary of Health and Family Services Secretary of Public Safety Secretary of Energy and Natural Resources Secretary of Education Secretary of Transportation and Infrastructure Secretary of Business Affairs In this portion of the speech, Braun also highlighted two executive orders he signed earlier this year, one of which ended work-from-home policies and required employees to return to the office to help “improve productivity and collaboration.” The other order eliminated diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives from the state government, something that Braun said will be replaced with a “level playing field of MEI: merit, excellence and innovation.” “Diversity and equality are hallmarks of what we believe as a country,” Braun said during the speech, “but these top-down DEI mandates led to division and inefficiency. MEI puts merit and results at the forefront, and everyone has the chance to get ahead with hard work.” Public Safety While Braun said it was important to make government more efficient, Braun said it was important to protect vital services, including police, fire and schools. During the speech, Braun said that police recruitment and retention for the Indiana State Police needs to be improved, including: Competitive salaries Cost of living adjustments More comprehensive benefits. Braun also highlighted Senate Bill 324, a piece of legislation written by Indiana State Senator Aaron Freeman, that would increase penalties to crimes related to fentanyl and methamphetamine, increase penalty levels of cases involving multiple battery cases and add minimum bail requirements for the release of a “repeat violent arrestee.” According to the general assembly’s website, the first reading for Senate Bill 324 initially occurred on Jan. 13 and was referred to the senate’s Committee on Corrections and Criminal Law. On Tuesday, the committee passed the bill and reassigned it to the senate’s Committee on Appropriations. Braun also took some time to speak on the immigration-related executive order he signed on Tuesday. According to previous reports, the order directed law enforcement agencies throughout Indiana to fully cooperate with the Immigration and Customs Enforcement agency, or ICE. At the time, Braun’s office said that the order “protects the public from the threats posed by illegal aliens” in the state, specifically those with criminal histories. During Wednesday’s speech, Braun said that Indiana will do whatever it can to help secure the southern border and “end this crisis.” “Let me be clear, Indiana will not be a safe haven for illegal immigration,” Braun said during the speech. “As the Trump administration begins to return illegal aliens with criminal records to their home countries, we will assist. A state should always put the safety of its own citizens first.” Economic Development In relation to economic development, Braun said that the state is “taking a new approach,” focusing on building workforce skills for Indiana residents to help match the high-value and high-wage jobs that need to be filled. Braun said that one of the ways the state will aim to achieve that is through the Hoosier Workforce Investment Tax Credit, something which Braun said would provide employers with a strong tax incentive to invest in skills training and development. Braun also said that the Indiana Economic Development Corporation will be restructured to “spread the benefits of economic development programs across the state.” Braun said the IEDC will also focus on “existing employers” and build on what other agencies are doing, including the newly established Office of Entrepreneurship and Innovation. Healthcare Along with property taxes, the other topic that Braun focused the majority of his gubernatorial campaign on was healthcare, an issue seen by some as “too tough to tackle.” “Hoosiers pay some of the highest healthcare costs in the country,” Braun said, “but we don’t get the best healthcare results. Based on experience taking on this issue in my own business, I developed by Freedom and Opportunity healthcare plan to lower prices, improve access and promote innovation in the industry.” According to previous reports, Braun said in his agenda that he aimed to address healthcare costs by enhancing price transparency requirements, something that Braun addressed in a series of healthcare-related executive orders he signed earlier this month. Some of Braun’s other healthcare executive orders include orders that direct the Indiana Department of Health to release terminated pregnancy reports, as well as orders to tackle surprise billing and high drug prices, as well as assess waste, fraud and abuse. A series of bills, including House Bill 1003, Senate Bill 3 and House Bill 1004, have already been filed as part of this year’s session, covering topics like price transparency, fighting fraud and making sure nonprofit hospitals “actually act like nonprofits.” “Hoosiers should be able to see what they’re going to have to pay before they receive care, and a medical bill... should never come as a surprise,” Braun said during the speech. Education The last topic that Braun covered during his speech was the importance of universal school choice in the state of Indiana, stressing his belief that parents are in charge of their own kids’ education. “Let’s put parents in the driver’s seat and kids first with universal school choice,” Braun said during the speech. “My budget proposal has the plan to get it done and with your help, we’ll win this historic victory for Hoosier parents and kids.” Braun also highlighted two bills, Senate Bill 146 and House Bill 1637, which covered two items that Braun spoke about during his campaign: the importance of increasing teacher pay and the establishment of the Office of School Safety. To conclude the speech, Braun said that his overall message to the Indiana General Assembly, as this year’s session continues, is “one of unity.” “We were all elected by Hoosiers to get results,” Braun said. “Will we treat this moment as a plateau, or as a foundation to build something truly exceptional? Now is the time to be bold and think out of the box. We can do it through big ideas, collaboration and above all: action.” | | | | | | PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE. | | NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE. WWW.INDIANA.GOP. | | 101 W. OHIO ST., SUITE 2200 INDIANAPOLIS, IN 46204 | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com | | |

name: Dyneco Gibson
subject: Want to learn more about how the courts affect your life? Join us TONIGHT for an exclusive virtual townhall
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund
body:
Good afternoon Friend,
We hope this invitation finds you well. Looking to continue our vital work providing civic education to our centered communities, we want to share this invitation from the affiliated organization, New Pennsylvania Project and Why Courts Matter - PA:
Join us today for our Why Courts Matter- 201 Virtual Town Hall :
Date: Thursday, January 30, 2025
Time: 6:30 – 8:00 PM (via Zoom)
Speakers: Kadida Kenner, Founding CEO, New Pennsylvania Project
Why Attend?
This town hall will focus on the importance of our state and federal courts, how they impact our daily lives, and how to engage in the state judicial election process, and protect the independence of our federal courts.
Please register today [https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZIufuGrqzojGtymEzpq4tBavgDUGYGt1yS0] , and feel free to share this with your friends and family.
Specifically, we will discuss:
1.- How federal judges are appointed and confirmed for a lifetime, and their long-term impact on our daily lives.
2.- The 2025 Pennsylvania judicial elections (including retention elections for three seats on the PA Supreme Court).
Your participation in state judicial elections can make a difference. A civically engaged electorate is crucial to ensuring fair and independent courts.
We look forward to having you with us as we work toward an informed and empowered electorate in 2025!
Please register at the link below, and invite a friend. We need more people to join the movement. We look forward to seeing you.
RSVP HERE [https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZIufuGrqzojGtymEzpq4tBavgDUGYGt1yS0?nvep=&hmac=&emci=82d413a2-29df-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&emdi=ea000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001&ceid=#/registration]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/Screenshot%202025-01-30%20114138.png]
We hope to see you there!
In Solidarity,
Dyneco Gibson
Director of Campaigns and Engagement
New PA Project Education Fund
dyneco@nppef.org ; (717) 847-1201
Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund
New PA Project Education Fund
PO Box 453
West Chester, PA 19381
United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/eTvv1eYlREi8TFpa7DUshg2?unsubscribedata=dicn5anNxIW4CGADWf2cJhxqD0zIgqcN%2FZUJu83PWVcKyw41fThXYVxf9qypveALQ3u%2FxyYBeghOqoBeFV8IgOvY3LiBEqtHMqX43uACcMR4DD6nLtHT%2FdrjZ2PzJcWzyjjA%2BK9jpIvtiVGzB8auRYeGqnh0rhZGSdF3auHynabrb%2FWf1%2BkEXSlxTPl2klcEnnGz5zasbcIqbRwTyoU7xtOWla8Cq3EYnDzmToY490j5cCGQThkzo94o8nwiCeGnm2QyBLrHZ2rxbdm6rd06e5S%2Fkg8%2BFiZsVKiKVIKdHM9BIytpZ8LZB%2FNLoFWcag9OdDkHnHHPRC11rN%2BuXLM6RF5Rf72UbHbrYAo4ogC%2Bxy6swu4bu2CtLQWRHc3UDJPVFcRZcRqsGOJvj4cmtGV7XQY14mVN87%2BFHgdbhOM%2BGEY%3D .

Wisconsin deserves better.

clean_body: Will you split a donation between EMILYs List and Judge Susan Crawford? | = Peter, Judge Susan Crawford's race for the Wisconsin state Supreme Court will be one of the most consequential elections of the year. She is running to fill a vacant seat that will determine the Court's majority — and whether the fundamental rights of Wisconsinites will be protected — for years to come. At a moment like this, we need fair and impartial justices like Susan Crawford who are committed to defending our fundamental rights. = | = Susan Crawford's opponent is anti-choice extremist Brad Schimel, who, as Wisconsin's attorney general, attacked reproductive freedom, let thousands of rape kits go untested, and fought to protect the GOP's gerrymandered congressional maps. Wisconsin deserves better. We can't let extremists like Schimel sit on our states' highest courts, especially when reproductive freedom increasingly hangs in the balance. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you, the EMILYs List State & Local Campaigns team = Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. name: Emily Randall
subject: On Trump's unlawful federal funding freeze
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress
body:
Let’s be absolutely clear: The President does not have the power to deny these critical services to the American people without the approval of Congress. A judge’s injunction stopped him for now, but we’re not taking our eyes off this for a second.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101247543/525363873/2145945666?refcode=RS_EM_FR_2025.01.30_TrumpFreeze_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZTA1ZDQyMC0yZGRmLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk1NzkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=O4KLiQNOBPPPwr456TCD0MiceWrGViN8FNhqRfnxvrI=&emci=5e04dd47-a1de-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&emdi=de05d420-2ddf-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&ceid=23483442) | Peter, This week, President Trump tried to singlehandedly gut the federal government with a reckless, unconstitutional order. Even though a federal judge blocked the order on Tuesday night, Trump’s actions threw the lives of millions of Americans into uncertainty and chaos. I’ve spent the last few days meeting with neighbors and community leaders across the 6th district — in Mason and Clallam, Pierce and Grays Harbor — many of whom rely on the critical lifelines cut off by Trump’s order: Head Start childcare, Medicaid, Indian Health Service, grants to small businesses, and so much more. We have already been in touch with over 100 community leaders to better understand the impact of this funding freeze. Let’s be absolutely clear: The President does not have the power to deny these critical services to the American people without the approval of Congress. A judge’s injunction stopped him for now, but we’re not taking our eyes off this for a second. **Our communities are already struggling. Our lives are already uncertain. It’s not too much to ask that our leaders work to alleviate our burdens, not add to them.** My team and I will continue to work on the issues facing working families. In service, — Emily

clean_body: Let’s be absolutely clear: The President does not have the power to deny these critical services to the American people without the approval of Congress. A judge’s injunction stopped him for now, but we’re not taking our eyes off this for a second. | Peter, This week, President Trump tried to singlehandedly gut the federal government with a reckless, unconstitutional order. Even though a federal judge blocked the order on Tuesday night, Trump’s actions threw the lives of millions of Americans into uncertainty and chaos. I’ve spent the last few days meeting with neighbors and community leaders across the 6th district — in Mason and Clallam, Pierce and Grays Harbor — many of whom rely on the critical lifelines cut off by Trump’s order: Head Start childcare, Medicaid, Indian Health Service, grants to small businesses, and so much more. We have already been in touch with over 100 community leaders to better understand the impact of this funding freeze. Let’s be absolutely clear: The President does not have the power to deny these critical services to the American people without the approval of Congress. A judge’s injunction stopped him for now, but we’re not taking our eyes off this for a second. Our communities are already struggling. Our lives are already uncertain. It’s not too much to ask that our leaders work to alleviate our burdens, not add to them. My team and I will continue to work on the issues facing working families. In service, — Emily = | | Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress Emily Randall for Congress P.O. clean_body: | | I asked once. I asked twice. Now, I'm asking again. That’s how essential it is that we meet tomorrow's End of Month goal if we want any hope of ensuring victory for our Democrats. = = | = = = = = Right now, Democrats are only THREE seats away from reclaiming the House majority. In a fight this close, the future of our country will come down to who has the resources to register voters, recruit top-tier candidates and deliver their winning message to voters. = = | = = | = = | = = Keep the faith, Hakeem Hakeem Jeffries is the Democratic Leader of the U.S. House of Representatives. Like many of us, Hakeem was raised by a working-class family; His mom was a social worker and his dad was a substance abuse counselor. Now, as the top Democrat in the House, he’s fighting to stop MAGA extremism and reclaim the majority for Democrats. Hakeem is committed to electing a record number of Democrats in the next election and beyond. But he can’t compete with Republicans’ special interest cash without our support. Is there any way you could chip in to help? = We know you’re getting a lot of emails, but that’s because Democrats have a clear path to winning back the House – and Hakeem isn’t letting a single second go to waste. Jeffries for Congress PO BOX 65322 Washington, DC 20035 United States | mailto:info@hakeemjeffries.com | | | Paid for by Jeffries for Congress

name: Hillsdale College via Victory News
subject: How should we elect the president?
body:
Please see a special message from our sponsor:

Unsubscribe
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xul=2qq8ny363wdaxf3eu1ztoj1l8h9amiu4ml783fg05hownh2i5ls2lq2tjw9s99w4n52qmx8gucu5hoene99hnr4umhqnvpqfkv75a1zjz&__stmp=sqww0f&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh

Please see a special message from our sponsor:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sqww0f&__onlt=t

Dear Fellow American,

I am enclosing a survey on a very urgent matter.

It will only take a moment of your time, but the topic is vital to our nation's future.

A movement is growing, led by progressives—but supported by many well-meaning Americans—to change the way we elect our president. In effect, it seeks to do away with the Electoral College as devised by the Framers of our Constitution.

That's why Hillsdale College is conducting a National Survey on Presidential Selection.

By completing this survey and returning it to me as soon as possible, you will help Hillsdale College more clearly understand the views of mainstream Americans concerning this issue—views we will make available to policymakers and opinion leaders.

You can complete the National Survey on Presidential Selection by using the link below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=17x64w94u6dz4n8mbbrr9ssqcthjq2ui45x7hflkwib3zrx6ic77m5ij9ii0ddcfog1wm8m93j52gg2o3ouqrg9ssyfr7ichm4jdh8ttob3kt6rdaknud3e9bvxrhwmz7cqgj49f8oarbyleonq0srqdv6qefu&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sqww0f&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sqww0f&__onlt=t

Do you think that most Americans understand how the Electoral College works and its role in preserving free and representative government?

YES
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sqww0f&__onlt=t

NO
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sqww0f&__onlt=t

For most of our nation's history, the Electoral College, as devised by the Framers of our Constitution, was not controversial. Indeed, it was considered essential to preserving free and representative government.

But today, more than a dozen states have joined together in an attempt to undermine this constitutional bulwark of liberty.

This movement to undermine the Constitution has grown largely because of the failure of America's schools to provide young people with a proper grounding in American civics—too many Americans simply don't understand the importance of the Constitution, including the Electoral College, to liberty.

Because of this, Hillsdale College has expanded its educational mission on behalf of liberty nationwide, directly teaching millions of Americans in all 50 states.

These efforts, which aim to counter the Left's domination over most of our schools, include:

Teaching millions of citizens nationwide through free online courses, such as "Constitution 101" and "The Great American Story: A Land of Hope;"

Reaching over six million households and businesses nationwide with Imprimis, Hillsdale's free digest of liberty;

Promoting constitutional education through social media outlets popular with young people;

Sending millions of pocket-size copies of America's founding documents to citizens and K-12 schools across America; and

Restoring excellence to K-12 education by helping establish and guiding classical K-12 schools nationwide and providing, free of charge, Hillsdale's American classical K-12 curriculum (including American civics) to thousands of American educators and homeschoolers.

Your answers to the National Survey on Presidential Selection will help Hillsdale form the most effective strategy to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty—including the importance of the Electoral College.

And this is especially important in this critical time for America's future.

So please share your views on this urgent topic by completing our National Survey on Presidential Selection and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few minutes of your time.

You can take the survey using this secure link I've provided below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=17x64w94u6dz4n8mbbrr9ssqcthjq2ui45ggkm7cudt0ghmpmppvi0d7lgy8qjjofo83pjcgul0lj62hv7x2m5xw6tywneptvhyaa7qrzb0bo1m9bwzkey37qczol6jyg03e71cydj0bqle8ln9264isytot9a&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sqww0f&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sqww0f&__onlt=t

And after you take your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Since 1844, Hillsdale has been "pursuing truth and defending liberty." Today, it does so through its extensive educational outreach efforts while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.

The support of private citizens like you is essential to our work.

Please represent your state and take the National Survey on Presidential Selection today. Thank you for your time and attention—and for your friendship and support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn

President, Hillsdale College

Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

body:
Before they’re gone

_**A message from our sponsor...**_ | | | [](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=e41a3b3d4ba445bda683cedeaf22e4c9&_e=5r0rqMKrWpgptgJs4vns-LqQqwouPiyHyLjU7AMJkbiHGIMUnqwt-Cazlz90uG_8fUmY_c9-ocTLR76qw0Yyyh5wpbsU5IqflChGkfo3jl6Ws2XAbSTStXGAECKfxFK8ukN8Dy6HQeX7oBedmIhFLJ10o_d0ecWe5RbWGBQZCWJ5NJX-8T53XQFAYmoehbN3uYOkAuqmqr75GU02KZqgFam-bExbOs_pwKrdWzWCcJ6ZHXLNbOQ1r-E0_VdzDlAsbmXDI1FXGuffI3Yj8aRMbTdBehS2UHWBsqXWTiuW7baCfTLCOyLxZFb3Ck0KvI5mQTP2JbpOcTyO-V6F5VPLmwtos_AarW7iuY5NDlYu4P3d7lQfXDU3rga8l_6PUAthwEz2m9QFnvSb4SUSGbMHrplj05zUCOT3CjTTCpBfbauDBmuP8E-Q4rsBk9Z_VdOE)

| | Dear Fellow American, World War II is the greatest armed conflict in human history, encompassing global fighting in unprecedented ways. We must preserve the lessons learned from this world war.
---
| | That’s why Hillsdale College has produced an online course entitled [“](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=e41a3b3d4ba445bda683cedeaf22e4c9&_e=5r0rqMKrWpgptgJs4vns-LqQqwouPiyHyLjU7AMJkbiHGIMUnqwt-Cazlz90uG_8fUmY_c9-ocTLR76qw0Yyyh5wpbsU5IqflChGkfo3jl6Ws2XAbSTStXGAECKfxFK8ukN8Dy6HQeX7oBedmIhFLJ10o_d0ecWe5RbWGBQZCWJ5NJX-8T53XQFAYmoehbN3uYOkAuqmqr75GU02KZqgFam-bExbOs_pwKrdWzWCcJ6ZHXLNbOQ1r-E0_VdzDlAsbmXDI1FXGuffI3Yj8aRMbTdBehS2UHWBsqXWTiuW7bZ9PZBm9h-zkp2EoIUBDhi18aPqMo1iV6w1eWuFMITYbg-WTWV0qMaEoMsLTS8tgYk23Ij7putv4YwCNhSQj-FJtzqM2ZM0PXyv-8kdMiA0K91sgkddj7x1i1ZosoT0M0DS7loeL4wgt2a7oOqkEJZE)**[The Second World Wars.”](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=e41a3b3d4ba445bda683cedeaf22e4c9&_e=5r0rqMKrWpgptgJs4vns-LqQqwouPiyHyLjU7AMJkbiHGIMUnqwt-Cazlz90uG_8fUmY_c9-ocTLR76qw0Yyyh5wpbsU5IqflChGkfo3jl6Ws2XAbSTStXGAECKfxFK8ukN8Dy6HQeX7oBedmIhFLJ10o_d0ecWe5RbWGBQZCWJ5NJX-8T53XQFAYmoehbN3uYOkAuqmqr75GU02KZqgFam-bExbOs_pwKrdWzWCcJ6ZHXLNbOQ1r-E0_VdzDlAsbmXDI1FXGuffI3Yj8aRMbTdBehS2UHWBsqXWTiuW7baHn6bEiOg2FoSRbXXD8cEBc7FzIYsiqx54_TkaqO-3InuSF_RaEsOOemQkNoqnOKaotACHzDixTjBhXPZCkg6fflFFVC1YDFBvDrAgyv8G1RQ11y8Y44InO1q7qpEnLgWJCfDA4cXNjXqPAbEQfigo) **And right now, you can receive your very own DVD box set of this course when you make a tax-deductible gift of $100 or more in support of our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty. This limited-edition DVD set provides an analysis from military historian **Victor Davis Hanson** of Allied and Axis investments and strategies in the air, by water, and on land that led to the Allies’ victory. This is one of the most popular Hillsdale courses of all time. And there are only a limited number of DVDs available. If you’d like to secure a DVD set before they’re all gone, you can have one shipped directly to your home for any gift of $100 or more to Hillsdale College.

body:
Minnesota's 5th district can't be bought
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=1475%2E791427%2E8HRuNh)

$1. That's the contribution Ilhan Omar is asking you to make today.
That's right — just $1.
$1 to keep Ilhan in the fight for issues most important to our movement in Washington, like Medicare for All, a Green New Deal, and student debt cancellation.
$1 to send a powerful message to the right-wing super PACs and their billionaire donors that Minnesota's 5th district can't be bought.
$1 because we've got just ONE day to go until the end of the month, and we have some important fundraising goals to reach before then.
The truth is that if everyone reading this email chipped in just $1 today, we'd CRUSH those goals and be well on our way to funding an unstoppable grassroots movement.
But not everyone chips in. That's why Ilhan's counting on supporters like you — who understand best what's at stake — to stand with us today.

name: Roy Cooper
subject: 65,000 votes hang in the balance
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
I need you to stand with me for our democracy.

|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101248920/525366359/823540626?refcode=ea.88857cd3-2edf-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&ms=ea.88857cd3-2edf-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ODg1N2NkMy0yZWRmLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk1NzkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8jwe6WGn1F0pPAjPqr02sgJWKrBqebpfwcPGpajZuYI=&emci=ebff3d09-1bdf-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&emdi=88857cd3-2edf-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&ceid=1049176)
This week, the Fourth Circuit Court of Appeals heard arguments on whether the Republican party can proceed with a challenge to throw out 65,000 legal votes in order to overturn Justice Allison Riggs’ victory in the November election. Justice Riggs won. Two recounts confirmed it. The ballots that North Carolina Republicans want to throw out are valid. This is a grave attack on our most cherished democratic values. **[North Carolina voters deserve to have their voices heard. If you can, please make a contribution now to power Justice Riggs’ campaign through these legal challenges and deliver the will of the voters.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101248923/525366360/-637652646?refcode=jan29&ms=ea.88857cd3-2edf-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ODg1N2NkMy0yZWRmLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk1NzkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8jwe6WGn1F0pPAjPqr02sgJWKrBqebpfwcPGpajZuYI=)** This baseless challenge was rejected by the NC State Board of Elections, but that didn’t stop Justice Riggs’ opponent. He’s even willing to disenfranchise our military service members and their families in order to grab power. We can’t let this stand. The past few weeks have been chaotic, but we can’t afford to lose our focus. The future of our democracy in North Carolina — and the United States — is at stake. | [DONATE NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101248925/525366361/-637652646?refcode=jan29&ms=ea.88857cd3-2edf-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ODg1N2NkMy0yZWRmLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk1NzkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8jwe6WGn1F0pPAjPqr02sgJWKrBqebpfwcPGpajZuYI=)
---
Thank you for your support. We will keep you updated as this case proceeds.
Roy Cooper

clean_body: I need you to stand with me for our democracy. | This week, the Fourth Circuit Court of Appeals heard arguments on whether the Republican party can proceed with a challenge to throw out 65,000 legal votes in order to overturn Justice Allison Riggs’ victory in the November election. Justice Riggs won. Two recounts confirmed it. The ballots that North Carolina Republicans want to throw out are valid. This is a grave attack on our most cherished democratic values. = This baseless challenge was rejected by the NC State Board of Elections, but that didn’t stop Justice Riggs’ opponent. He’s even willing to disenfranchise our military service members and their families in order to grab power. We can’t let this stand. The past few weeks have been chaotic, but we can’t afford to lose our focus. The future of our democracy in North Carolina — and the United States — is at stake. | = Thank you for your support. We will keep you updated as this case proceeds. Roy Cooper = mailto:info@roycooper.com | Cooper for North Carolina PO Box 1190 Raleigh, NC 27602 United States Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. name: Mandela Barnes
subject: Will you help me?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Long Run PAC, P
body:
#
I am going to be completely transparent today.
$2,750. That's the amount of money we still need to raise in the next 36 hours. Let me quickly explain why:
Tomorrow at midnight marks the end of January and our critical end-of-month deadline. By the end of the month, we need to gauge the momentum of our movement and start to decide which candidates we want to support this cycle.
Without reaching that last $2,750, it’s going to be tough for us to continue our work supporting changemaking candidates. Because the goals we set are the absolute minimum we need to make sure this movement can continue running and be sustained through this first phase of Trump’s presidency.
We are not aiming for an unrealistic amount — we just need the funding that will help us continue supporting candidates who will decide whether or not Democrats can take back the House and even the Senate from the GOP and have a powerful check on Trump.
So, what do you say?
[**Can you help us reach our $2,750 goal before our critical fundraising deadline tomorrow night? House GOP Leader Tony McCombie Punishes Conservative GOP Members, Threatens to Remove Them from Caucus Meeting by Force for Exposing Tactics (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/01/house-gop-leader-tony-mccombie-punishes-conservative-gop-members-threatens-to-remove-them-from-caucus-meeting-by-force-for-exposing-tactics.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=15587a43af-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/01/house-gop-leader-tony-mccombie-punishes-conservative-gop-members-threatens-to-remove-them-from-caucus-meeting-by-force-for-exposing-tactics.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=15587a43af-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823
(Tony McCombie/Tony McCombie official website)
By Illinois Review

Embattled Illinois House Republican Minority Leader Tony McCombie is acting like a tyrant and punishing conservative House GOP members who remain critical of her and did not attend the swearing-in ceremony at the state capitol where she was lauded as the House GOP leader – threatening to have capitol police remove them from a caucus meeting if they don’t play by her rules.

McCombie, who is facing mounting criticism from Republican voters after her recent vote in favor of
expanding transgender patients rights (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/01/il-house-gop-leader-tony-mccombie-joins-woke-left-supports-insurance-coverage-for-mens-cervical-exams-and-womens-prostate-screenings.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=15587a43af-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823) was revealed – has removed constituent services and legislative assistants from five House GOP members who decided to have their own swearing-in ceremony on inauguration day.

The move prohibits elected representatives from providing service to their constituents. The five members affected include State Representatives Chris Miller, Blaine Wilhour, Brad Halbrook, Adam Niemerg and Martin McLaughlin.

name: Eric Sorensen
subject: Here’s why early fundraising is so important.
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101251698/525369387/1514584251?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS85MzkwNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTJjN2E2NC0zMGRmLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk1NzkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=592AQFrThoP7DqdKSBwh7JQRFlw2hH5d1wQFjqwR22E=)
Let me be direct, Peter. I'm reaching out to ask you to make a [**$3 donation**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101251702/525369394/1514584251?refcode=em-1.30.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS85MzkwNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTJjN2E2NC0zMGRmLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk1NzkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=592AQFrThoP7DqdKSBwh7JQRFlw2hH5d1wQFjqwR22E=) ahead of tomorrow’s end-of-month fundraising deadline. To be completely transparent, we're pacing behind our fundraising target this month, so I want to briefly explain why your donation today would mean so much: **The good news is that despite the dysfunction from the White House making the headlines, we've actually been delivering early wins for our communities****in IL-17.** We've dug right in on casework around the district, and I just joined the House Agricultural and Armed Services committees where I can have a hand in the policy that affects thousands of district jobs (more on that soon). **Unfortunately, actually doing the job of a Congressman comes at a campaign cost.** I've had less time (read: none) to fundraise for my reelection effort, and that's not ideal given that National Republicans have already (yes, already) started putting out lies about me ahead of 2026. Tallying strong fundraising numbers now will go a long way in warding off Republican misinformation and will even help prevent super PACs from attacking me. They'll see us as too formidable and not worth the expense. That's why this goal is so important: [**Will you chip in a donation to help me meet the mark on my end-of-month goal? Please know that I only ask because it's critical to our campaign and flipping the House in 2026.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101251707/525369400/1514584251?refcode=em-1.30.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS85MzkwNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTJjN2E2NC0zMGRmLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk1NzkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=592AQFrThoP7DqdKSBwh7JQRFlw2hH5d1wQFjqwR22E=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Chip in $10 Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101251715/525369403/1514584251?refcode=em-1.30.25-all-b&express_lane=true&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS85MzkwNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTJjN2E2NC0zMGRmLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk1NzkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=592AQFrThoP7DqdKSBwh7JQRFlw2hH5d1wQFjqwR22E=)

It means so much to me that our work is powered by folks like you who chip in on emails just like this one. As we head into the next election, we do so knowing that our efforts will hinge on your ongoing support.
Thanks for your support,
-Eric

clean_body: | = Let me be direct, Peter. I'm reaching out to ask you to make a = ahead of tomorrow’s end-of-month fundraising deadline. To be completely transparent, we're pacing behind our fundraising target this month, so I want to briefly explain why your donation today would mean so much: The good news is that despite the dysfunction from the White House making the headlines, we've actually been delivering early wins for our communitiesin IL-17. We've dug right in on casework around the district, and I just joined the House Agricultural and Armed Services committees where I can have a hand in the policy that affects thousands of district jobs (more on that soon). Unfortunately, actually doing the job of a Congressman comes at a campaign cost. I've had less time (read: none) to fundraise for my reelection effort, and that's not ideal given that National Republicans have already (yes, already) started putting out lies about me ahead of 2026. Tallying strong fundraising numbers now will go a long way in warding off Republican misinformation and will even help prevent super PACs from attacking me. They'll see us as too formidable and not worth the expense. That's why this goal is so important: = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = It means so much to me that our work is powered by folks like you who chip in on emails just like this one. As we head into the next election, we do so knowing that our efforts will hinge on your ongoing support. Thanks for your support, -Eric = | Eric Sorensen is a meteorologist born and raised in Rockford, IL representing IL-17 in Congress. name: Reagan Dunn
subject: One Simple Decision
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Reagan Dunn
body:
One decision can make all the difference | | https://us5.forward-to-friend.com/forward?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id;=77fbd65fe9&e;=77eef70c61 | Is this email not displaying correctly?
[View it in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/c34ee468ff25/one-simple-decision?e=77eef70c61).
---|---
| [](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=ea154a17c0&e=77eef70c61)
---
| | Friends,

Leadership is often about making tough decisions. As your last line of defense against extremist downtown Seattle policies, I work hard to make the tough decisions necessary to keep our neighborhoods safe and prosperous.

Not too long ago, when King County Officials proposed closing our youth jail and releasing violent rapists and murderers into unsecured respite homes in our communities, I made the decision to be a voice of reason against extremism. I fought back against that dangerous policy proposal with everything I had. After a lot of hard work, we succeeded in keeping our communities safe by reversing that disastrous proposal and passing legislation at the King County Council to keep the youth jail open!

But here's the thing, it wasn't just my decision to fight back against this policy that led to a victory for our communities. It was the courageous decision of many of my supporters, like you, standing up and voicing your support for my legislation that made the difference.

**_Would you be willing to make a courageous decision again and support for my campaign by rushing a donation of_** **_$15, $25, or even $75 before January 31st? _** [**DONATE HERE**](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=677c5cabaf&e=77eef70c61)
One simple decision - not just by you, and not just by me, but by all of us collectively - to stand up and fight for common sense can make all the difference in preserving our region for future generations.

**Would you ****[make the decision to stand with me](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=fb1dfc8963&e=77eef70c61)****and support my campaign for a return to common sense****and safety****in King County today****?**

Our January deadline is fast approaching. Your decision to donate before **_MIDNIGHT ON JANUARY 31 st_ **could make all the difference in winning this campaign and ensuring common sense prevails in King County. [**YOU CAN DONATE RIGHT HERE!**](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=e0afcfcf3d&e=77eef70c61)

Thank you so much for your support! Your willingness to do so makes all the difference!

Reagan Dunn

---
| | [Follow on Twitter](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=586d3bd272&e=77eef70c61) | [ on Facebook](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=d793b66dfe&e=77eef70c61) |
---

name: With Honor Action
subject: Ukrainian Warfighters Hold Event at USA House
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WITH HONOR ACTION, INC
body:
| | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/fc9cd2ddee88/2-acts-and-a-vp-nominee-14051313?e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| | | |
---
| | | | Friends,

With Honor Action was proud to co-host the inaugural USA House in Davos, Switzerland this past week to promote U.S. interests on the world stage while fostering innovation and global cooperation.

During events at the USA House, we were honored to have been joined by Ukrainian warfighters from the frontlines. Check out the video from the USA House below to hear more about Ukraine’s fight for democracy and our work alongside them.

name: THREE SPECIAL ELECTIONS [From House GOP]
subject: Of all the emails you've received today, this is the MOST important!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
Our Conservative House Majority – and the fate of President Trump’s legislative agenda – hangs in the balance.
If Democrats take the House, they can stonewall EVERY America First Agenda item President Trump tries to pass through Congress.
| |
---
| | | Of all the emails you've received today, this is BY FAR the most important. Why? Because...

_We 're about to have a NEW Special Election in NY-21 for Elise Stefanik's seat, a NEW Special Election in FL-06 for Mike Waltz's seat, and a NEW Special Election in FL-01 for Matt Gaetz's seat!_

**Remember:** We have a RAZOR-THIN Majority in the House - so if we lose these Special Elections, Democrats could take control of the House.

**Remember:** If they take the House, they can stonewall EVERY America First Agenda item President Trump tries to pass through Congress.

**Remember:** If they jam up Trump's agenda, everything we fought so hard for this year will completely COLLAPSE.

**_[Friend- we need AT LEAST 20,000 contributions before the end of the month to maximize the chances of Republicans winning all three and stop the Democrats from taking control of our House Majority.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=f237c5af9b8f44fe9ccf10ec998bbacf&_e=VvfK6I-dzl_bep0XhaYlQVEDFIcYKAY38qQCtM6feP0fdFImByA_6VQ8gf7T7JPESd2JGmZyl_zGmST4qbW_KlNjsnL4DxuA6oOPlkV2Z_Am3PJPtqAGXscjcSGnyUAxEHbnbFWwuw1gX93Zzg3gnfzygBOy2Mh9B6mmEzx1TfxMg1WXlrfhRp4P-Ee6crDJlA-o5S-nDf88EaRakaT8zet_Q0_tMHRsuEW4MQXrUv9nHxgN-eFuThHr2X7C8_jYQ7dwnmJNHMEa-_Cp7WTEYg8Ze3TSR-0BNa-Fidr68-CY1hZsfAsod4vYqSCAkd5t04_DNt4noXsBmuD9fQaovVWQRlTgKNB8kwurYYeqeY4BlHiBYd_gsfA1WcD5gxHEZcJ15i4SpM8e9edPOuFTSg%3D%3D "Friend – we need AT LEAST 20,000 contributions before the end of the month to maximize the chances of Republicans winning all three and stop the Democrats from taking control of our House Majority.")_**

If there was ever a moment to pitch in a few bucks, that moment is right now. Our Conservative House Majority - and the fate of President Trump's legislative agenda - hangs in the balance.

Listen -

_This deadline was important before but now that our House Majority may depend on winning three Special Elections ..._

**THIS IS THE MOST IMPORTANT DEADLINE YET! **

Majority Leader Steve Scalise agreed that 20,000 more contributions is the bare minimum we need to help jumpstart our efforts to hold FL-01, FL-06, and NY-21.

Every contribution helps us refuel our voter contact budgets, recruit volunteers, and get our positive message out to voters - that's how we win key races like these.

name: Ruben Gallego
subject: They need to get their s**t together.
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
#

Peter —
A lot of you signed on, spoke out, and took action when the Trump administration tried to freeze $3 trillion in federal funding earlier this week.
Thanks to you — and to the states and organizations that filed lawsuits against the administration — that freeze is blocked. For now, at least.
That means that the folks I heard from in Arizona, organizations that provide housing and food for homeless veterans are going to get their funding. It means elderly folks who rely on Meals on Wheels will get what they need. That matters.
Now, a few things stuck out to me during that whole process, like the lack of clarity in that memo in the first place. Another was the uncertainty the President forced on millions of people who need and have earned help. And, to put it bluntly, the degree to which this administration simply does not have their s**t together.
It’s ridiculous, honestly, and it’s going to be a long four years.
But, beyond that, one other thing stuck out. Millions of us joined together and united to stop him — and, for now at least, it worked.
This team played a part in that, and it reinforced how important everything we’ve built together really is. This team is special, and I haven’t forgotten that.
But we’re not done. Trump’s team is regrouping, and more tough moments are on their way every day. I just hope you know that I’ll be doing what I can to push back.
Thanks, Peter. Means a lot that you’re here.
Ruben
Before you go...
[**Consider making a recurring monthly donation to Ruben Gallego’s campaign.**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-em-jan25%3Frecurring%3Dtrue.SO4wUz&refcode=em250130-1-90&t=1&akid=3686%2E734936%2E9yFTRC)
Donations like this are how we fight back. Every donation, no matter how small, makes a difference. What matters most is that you’re supporting Ruben at a time when he really needs our help.
[DONATE](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-em-jan25%3Frecurring%3Dtrue.SO4wUz&refcode=em250130-1-90&t=2&akid=3686%2E734936%2E9yFTRC)

Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
Use of military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform do not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails like this are a critical tool for our campaign's fundraising and organizing efforts. If you'd like to receive only the most important emails going forward, [tell us at this link](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/signup/fewer_emails/?source=fewer-emails&t=3&akid=3686%2E734936%2E9yFTRC). Or, if you need to, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=3686%2E734936%2E9yFTRC). If you would like to support Ruben's campaign for Senate, please consider [making a donation today.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ruben-em-footer?refcode=em-footer&t=5&refcode2=3686_734936_9yFTRC&akid=3686%2E734936%2E9yFTRC)

|

Peter,

The new year has already presented many challenges – from the devastating wildfires close to home to Republicans taking control of Congress and the White House.

And while it might seem far off, especially after the inauguration and Trump’s flurry of executive orders, I’m keeping a close eye on 2026.

**As a reminder, we were one of the few Democratic campaigns to flip a House seat from red to blue in a difficult election year – and we did with just over 2.5% of the vote. That makes us an early target for national Republicans.**

We’re preparing _right now_ to protect this seat as extremists look to expand their grip on Congress. You helped us make history last cycle, and it’s essential that this team sticks together if we’re going to do it again.

**[Will you chip in a one-time or recurring donation right now? $3 would be incredible, but any amount will help us hit our first fundraising goal as a member of Congress and set the tone in the new year.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101253703/525375504/-2002425095?refcode=em-1.30.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZWU2M2UwNi0zNGRmLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk1NzkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8BqVjTEIC5V-9idPHmvMS814l6POJTPVfccKO-pF74s=)**
